title: Evangelion seems a lot more real than most real robot shows
display_title: Evangelion seems a lot more real than most real robot shows
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion seems a lot more real than most real robot shows             Quote Post by GAP » Sun Mar 28, 2010 12:39 am Just as the title says, Evangelion is a lot more real more 'real' than a lot of real robot shows. Don't me wrong, those shows especially Gundam are real and they deal with themes in a mature way but it seels like the further I get across them, the less real they are to me. Evangelion is not necessarily all that realistic either but i seems a lot more real than any of the latest shows I had seen, Shinji goes through some serious stuff neither were any of the cast instant piloting experts but still it just seems more real than Gundam despite being a Super Robot show. am I wrong isd this assumption?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Mar 28, 2010 12:43 am There's such a thing as "Robot Show" and "Super Robot Show". On a scale, Evangelion is sort of on the in-between, but closer to "Robot Show". As for the characters, they're a lot more real and carry more depth than most characters in other robot shows, regular or super, because the main focus of Eva is, in fact, the characters and not the robots.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Mar 28, 2010 7:56 am lolwut

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Sun Mar 28, 2010 10:44 am Xard wrote: lolwut

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sun Mar 28, 2010 12:00 pm By "real" are you referencing the live action scenes in EoE? Because if so, I would say yes, Eva is more "real" than most robot animu series.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Sun Mar 28, 2010 1:35 pm Not just the live action scenes but the characters as well although they were exaggerated in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Mar 28, 2010 1:41 pm What's this topic's point?

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun Mar 28, 2010 1:43 pm ^I believe the OP is talking about how NGE is more realstic then most mecha shows like Gundam, and Ideon.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun Mar 28, 2010 2:14 pm I think that NGE seems rather "real" because aside from having good characters, it also presents a complex environment that goes beyond the whole "giant mech" theme. For example, there's that episode where Fuyutsuki is giving his account of the post-2I events, and there's a lot going on that doesn't have to do with the mechs. In additions there's a lot of infrastructure and military hardware that is constantly present throughout the series, as well as other background features that make the show seem more "real life-esque". Most other animes tend to overlook these details, and I'm actually really glad that Evangelion opted to include them. It's a really important part of the atmosphere. On a tangent about the military units, I thought it was interesting how they play a strictly passive role until the end, when the JSSDF pwns NERV. I really liked how Anno turned something that was mainly a thematic motif into an actual plot driver. What's this topic's point?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Mar 28, 2010 2:19 pm It's amazing what a little bit of character subtext will do for mecha animes, isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Mar 28, 2010 2:25 pm Gundam is closer to 'real' that eva, speaking only of the series namesakes. Mass-produced mechanized infantry fighting suits (though the scale is way off) are certainly on the horizon. Biologically human nuke-proof magical giants, not so much.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Mar 28, 2010 2:40 pm NGE is about as realistic as The Magic School Bus.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sun Mar 28, 2010 2:43 pm You made a good point there, GAP. Though it's true that some events in Evangelion will never happen, it's not as cartoonish as it looks. It has seriousness, issues and emotions that relate to today's modern life, and is, of course, beyond any mecha shows, which is what makes Evangelion so fascinating.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Sun Mar 28, 2010 2:45 pm I cannot say the series is realistic(although some of the characters truly are), but I will not say it's unrealistic. Strange shit happens everyday.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Mar 28, 2010 2:50 pm Don't me wrong, those shows especially Gundam are real and they deal with themes in a mature way but it seels like the further I get across them, the less real they are to me.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Sun Mar 28, 2010 7:15 pm Defectron wrote: "Real Robot" anime is just super robot anime with pretentions towards realism. Yeah the plots seem more realisitc since real robot shows try to steer away from stuff like alien invaders, but most of the science behind the shows isn't hard sc ifi like a lot of people like to pretend it is.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sun Mar 28, 2010 8:15 pm CHARACTERS: Real in an almost unsettling way. Extremely identifiable if not mirror images of many people. SITUATIONS: I, for one, only drive my giant mecha to school every day. Engaging in battles against manifestations of metaphysical beings, humph! Man has much better use for his technology. In other words, apart from the fact that it is animation, SHITS NOT REAL MUDDAFUCKA.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun Mar 28, 2010 11:11 pm GAP wrote: Is that so? I always thought it was based on real science although that is ot entirely untrue, I did know that half the action in those shows were unrealistic.

[]:             Quote Post by zlink64 » Mon Mar 29, 2010 6:09 am Lol "Eva keeps it real"

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 29, 2010 10:17 am LeoXiao wrote: A good example is Full Metal Panic (a "Real robot" show) where machines the size of fighter jets can do kung fu. This is totally implausible when you consider how fast the turret of a tank turns (not very fast).

Don't me wrong, those shows especially Gundam are real and they deal with themes in a mature way but it seels like the further I get across them, the less real they are to me. Evangelion is not necessarily all th
